Title: Molecular and phenotypic characterization of Als1 and Als2 mutations conferring tolerance to acetolactate synthase herbicides in soybean

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W4-4 mutant line (mutant allele); wild-type comparison in Williams 82 genome annotation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetolactate synthase catalytic subunit; acetohydroxyacid synthase; thiamine pyrophosphate-dependent enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common upstream step supplying valine/leucine/isoleucine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first common step in branched-chain amino acid synthesis, forming acetolactate/related acetohydroxyacid intermediates from pyruvate-derived substrates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step stated as key enzyme inhibited by herbicides; rate-limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in W4-4 leaves; RT-qPCR indicates relatively low transcript abundance versus eIF-4A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in first trifoliate leaves of four-week-old plants; expression similar to Als2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted genomic sequencing; cDNA cloning; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Post-emergence application of ALS-inhibiting herbicides under greenhouse conditions; 16 h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P178S mutant Als1 improves soybean tolerance to multiple ALS-inhibiting herbicides, especially several sulfonylureas and flucarbazone; in combination with Als2 gives synergistically improved herbicide tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele sequencing; full-length alignment; cDNA cloning; RT-qPCR; phenotypic dose-response assays across 11 herbicides; isobole interaction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful causative SNP for marker-assisted breeding and deployment into elite soybean germplasm to improve tolerance to ALS-inhibiting herbicides
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No increased sensitivity reported to imazethapyr or cloransulam methyl; other metabolic or growth tradeoffs not reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Als1 corresponds to Glyma04g37270.1 and carries a P178S substitution in W4-4. 2. The mutant transcript is expressed in soybean leaves. 3. Als1 alone increases tolerance to several ALS herbicides. 4. Combined with Als2, tolerance is synergistically enhanced for several herbicide chemistries.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation corresponds to P197S in Arabidopsis numbering; gene is on chromosome 4 and unlinked to Als2

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W4-4 mutant line (mutant allele); wild-type comparison in Williams 82 genome annotation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetolactate synthase catalytic subunit; acetohydroxyacid synthase; thiamine pyrophosphate-dependent enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common upstream step supplying valine/leucine/isoleucine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first common step in branched-chain amino acid synthesis, forming acetolactate/related acetohydroxyacid intermediates from pyruvate-derived substrates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step stated as key enzyme inhibited by herbicides; rate-limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in W4-4 leaves; RT-qPCR indicates relatively low transcript abundance versus eIF-4A and approximately similar level to Als1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in first trifoliate leaves of four-week-old plants; Als2/Als1 expression ratio ~1.30, interpreted as essentially same
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted genomic sequencing; cDNA cloning; RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Post-emergence application of ALS-inhibiting herbicides under greenhouse conditions; 16 h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: W560L mutant Als2 improves soybean tolerance to imazapyr and multiple sulfonylurea herbicides and flucarbazone; in combination with Als1 gives synergistically improved tolerance to several ALS inhibitor classes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele sequencing; full-length alignment; cDNA cloning; RT-qPCR; phenotypic dose-response assays across 11 herbicides; isobole interaction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful causative SNP for marker-assisted breeding and germplasm improvement for broad ALS-herbicide tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No increased sensitivity reported to imazethapyr or cloransulam methyl; other metabolic or growth tradeoffs not reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Als2 corresponds to Glyma06g17790.1 and carries a W560L substitution in W4-4. 2. The mutant transcript is expressed in soybean leaves. 3. Als2 alone increases tolerance to several ALS herbicide families. 4. Combined with Als1, tolerance is synergistically enhanced for multiple herbicides.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation corresponds to W574L in Arabidopsis numbering; gene is on chromosome 6 and unlinked to Als1
